findings.evidence: `rg -n "@\[simp\]|complexLinearPartLinearMap_add_complexAntilinearPartLinearMap" code/TauCeti/LinearAlgebra/ComplexLinearPart.lean` shows `@[simp]` on the pointwise theorem at lines 239-240, while the operator identity at lines 248-250 has no attribute.
findings.fix: Add `@[simp]` to this theorem so downstream goals involving `(complexLinearPartLinearMap J J' + complexAntilinearPartLinearMap J J')` reduce to `LinearMap.id` without unfolding the operators.
findings.issue: `complexLinearPartLinearMap_add_complexAntilinearPartLinearMap` is the characteristic normal form for the public projection operators, but it is not marked `@[simp]`.

summary: The pointwise decomposition is now annotated, but the exposed linear-operator form is still missing the same normal-form automation. Since the PR publicly exposes `complexLinearPartLinearMap` and `complexAntilinearPartLinearMap`, their characteristic sum-to-identity lemma should simplify directly.
